View my work Get in touch

About

I'm a customer-obsessed engineer who cares as much about why something is being built as how it gets built, and whether it actually solves the right problem for the people using it.

My work spans the full stack, from AWS infrastructure and backend pipelines to the Angular frontends that traders and compliance teams use every day. I have led cross-functional migrations, partnered with infrastructure teams on high-stakes systems, and built the kind of trust that makes complex projects actually move.

What drives me is clear communication and proximity to the end user. Whether I am breaking down a monitoring architecture for a non-technical audience or aligning stakeholders across three teams, I stay focused on the outcome and the person on the other end.

Based in New York City and currently pursuing my M.Eng. in Engineering Management at Vanderbilt University, which sharpens how I think about systems, teams, and tradeoffs at scale.

How I Work

Two modes, one engineer

Cloud Infra ⬡

Platform & Infrastructure

Deep technical execution

  • Designing and owning AWS-native architectures: EC2, Lambda, S3, CloudWatch, auto-scaling
  • Building observability pipelines that close visibility gaps before they become outages
  • Automating CI/CD, deployment templates, and cost optimization scripts
  • Working across infra, security, and network teams on high-availability systems
  • Turning raw data (PySpark, Databricks, ETL) into operational intelligence
Forward Deployed ◈

Customer-Embedded Engineering

Shipping in the field

  • Embedding with stakeholders to understand workflows before writing a line of code
  • Translating ambiguous requirements into scoped, shippable solutions quickly
  • Leading pilots end-to-end from prototype to adoption across 19+ teams at Fidelity
  • Communicating technical tradeoffs to non-technical decision-makers through clear narrative
  • Iterating in the open: gathering live feedback, adjusting, and shipping again
  • Bringing a storyteller's instinct to stakeholder updates, docs, and cross-team alignment

Selected Projects

Things I've made

01

Book Club AI Host

I founded a recurring book club and noticed a real gap: planning good discussions is surprisingly hard, and generic prompts do not account for who is in the room. So I built an AI-powered tool on Create.xyz that generates dynamic, audience-specific discussion questions based on the book and the group. I ran the MVP live at a session, gathered feedback on the spot, and iterated the UI based on what people actually needed. The project reinforced something I bring to every engineering problem: the best solutions come from being in the room with users, not just imagining them.

Ask me about it →
AI/ML No-code User Research Create.xyz Community
02

Anomaly Detection Pipeline

Architected a PySpark-based anomaly detection system on Databricks, integrated with AWS S3 and CloudWatch. Closed 12 monitoring blind spots across a 200+ user platform and meaningfully reduced production outages.

PySpark Databricks AWS Observability
03

Cloud Cost Optimizer

Led an initiative at Fidelity to reduce AWS spend using EC2 spot instances, auto-scaling policies, and Python automation. Saved $15K annually and reinvested gains into enhanced reporting for business stakeholders.

AWS EC2 Python FinOps Auto-scaling
04

Compliance Trading Platform

Modernized a high-volume compliance trading UI at Fidelity. Redesigned workflows to cut task completion time by 50%, implemented Cucumber testing to reduce defects by 35%, and managed a two-month cross-team migration solo.

Angular Cucumber Compliance Scrum
05

Cloud Monitoring Dashboard

Built a real-time Angular dashboard with REST API integrations to monitor 100+ enterprise cloud applications. Enabled the cloud team to identify and resolve outages faster, with automated testing reducing manual error by 40%.

Angular REST APIs OpenStack Testing

Beyond Engineering

The other things I do

Social Media Manager

The Sewing Tin

The Sewing Tin is a sold-out NYC supper club serving a six-course desi tasting menu with cocktail pairings, rooted in South Asian nostalgia and identity. Inspired by the iconic blue tin that immigrant families repurposed into sewing kits, each dinner is as much about storytelling as it is about food.

I manage their social media presence, crafting content strategy and copy for a brand where the story is the product. Brand partnerships have included Johnnie Walker Blue Label and Royal Dansk. Tickets regularly sell out within minutes of dropping.

Visit The Sewing Tin →
11K+ Instagram followers
<15min Typical ticket sellout time
30+ Intimate seats per dinner

The same instinct that makes a great stakeholder update makes a great caption for a sold-out dinner: know your audience, lead with the story, earn the action.
